workshoptitle: Build a Recipe App with Oracle Backend for Firebase — iOS

tutorials.title: Introduction
tutorials.description: Review the learner journey and workshop architecture for the iOS RecipeShare app.

tutorials.title: Setup Oracle Backend for Firebase
tutorials.description: Install the runtime, download the local stack, start it, and verify the workshop services are running.

tutorials.title: Lab 1: Create Your First Project
tutorials.description: Open the console, create your first project, and set the starter security rules.

tutorials.title: Lab 2: Connect the iOS App
tutorials.description: Clone the repo, open the Xcode project, register an iOS app, paste the config, and verify the connection in the iOS Simulator.

tutorials.title: Lab 3: Authentication
tutorials.description: Add sign-up, sign-in, and sign-out to the iOS app using the Fusabase auth module.

tutorials.title: Lab 4: Read Recipe Data
tutorials.description: Implement loadRecipes in SwiftUI to query recipes with filters and sorting, then seed demo data.

tutorials.title: Lab 5: Write Recipe Data
tutorials.description: Implement createRecipe and addRecipeRating in SwiftUI to write data as an authenticated user.

tutorials.title: Lab 6: Photo Upload
tutorials.description: Upload recipe photos from the iOS photo picker and display them using the Fusabase storage module.

tutorials.title: Lab 7: Security Rules
tutorials.description: Replace the permissive starter rule with auth-based access controls.
